name: Saudi Arabia Pavilion
summary: Saudi Arabia signed its participation contract in April 2026, with a vision built around play as a pathway to opportunity — culture, sport and creative industries empowering individuals and communities.

about: Saudi Arabia signed its participation contract for Expo Belgrade 2027 in April 2026, marking what its Commissioner General called the transition from planning into the full execution phase. The participation is led by Rakan Tarabzoni, Deputy Minister of Economy and Planning for International Economic Affairs and the Kingdom's Commissioner General for Expo Belgrade 2027.

The Kingdom's stated vision focuses on showcasing play as a pathway to opportunity, and the role of culture, sport and creative industries in empowering individuals and building thriving communities — themes closely aligned with Vision 2030. Saudi Arabia says it will work with the Expo organizer and participating countries to deliver a pavilion and programme fostering collaboration, cultural exchange and people-to-people engagement throughout the Expo. Design and budget details have not been published.

facts.value: Participation contract signed April 2026 — execution phase

faqs.answer: Showcasing play as a pathway to opportunity, and the role of culture, sport and creative industries in empowering individuals and building thriving communities — themes closely aligned with Vision 2030.

faqs.answer: Rakan Tarabzoni, Deputy Minister of Economy and Planning for International Economic Affairs and the Kingdom's Commissioner General for Expo Belgrade 2027. Pavilion design and budget have not been published.
